message:
style(schema): use unwrap_or_else for Priority::default lookup (clippy or_fun_call)

Tiny clippy fix on the v7 content-hash rebuild loop — swap
`unwrap_or(Priority::default().0)` for
`unwrap_or_else(|| Priority::default().0)`. `Priority::default()`
isn't a free constant (it's a function call), so `unwrap_or` would
evaluate it on every row including when the stored value parses
successfully. `unwrap_or_else` defers the call to the None branch.

Negligible perf delta on any realistic DB, but it silences
`clippy::or_fun_call` and matches the existing idiom used for the
other row-parse defaults in the same block (e.g.
`IssueType::default().as_str()` via `unwrap_or_else(...)`).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
